summary: Shadow republish of @datastructures-js/priority-queue with aliased dependency redirect
details: This package republishes the source of @datastructures-js/priority-queue under the @torbeck scope while preserving the original author and copyright headers. Its package.json uses npm dependency aliasing to remap `@datastructures-js/heap` to `@torbeck/heap@4.3.8` (`"@datastructures-js/heap": "npm:@torbeck/heap@4.3.8"`), so the unmodified `require('@datastructures-js/heap')` call in src/priorityQueue.js silently loads code from the @torbeck-controlled shadow package instead of the legitimate upstream. The wrapper code is clean, but installing this package transitively pulls @torbeck/heap@4.3.8 into the install tree on every `npm install` and loads it on every require of priority-queue. The alias-substitution shape is engineered so a code-level review of the wrapper matches the legitimate package while a different, same-author package is the actual dependency satisfying the require — the canonical namespace-abuse / dependency-confusion pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
